items.label: McIlroy Headlines:
items.body: Rory McIlroy is the clear headliner at Quail Hollow, where he has repeatedly played well and arrives as the biggest name in the field.

items.label: Signature Event Pressure:
items.body: The Truist Championship is a 72-player Signature Event with no cut, a $20 million purse, and a deep elite field, so the margin for error is small all week.

items.label: Quail Hollow Returns:
items.body: The event is back at its traditional Charlotte home after a one-year break for the PGA Championship, restoring one of the Tour’s most familiar big-stage setups.

items.label: Masters Hangover Watch:
items.body: Several top players are coming in off Augusta, which makes this a good week to watch who keeps momentum and who looks a little flat.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Sepp Straka returns as the defending champion after winning last year’s edition.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+640):
items.body: The betting favorite and the most obvious fit for Quail Hollow, with five prior wins in Charlotte and a course profile that rewards his length and high-end driving.

items.label: Cameron Young (+890):
items.body: A powerful, high-upside option whose driving can turn Quail Hollow into a scoring opportunity if the ball control is there.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1275):
items.body: A steady major-championship caliber player whose balanced game travels well to a demanding Signature Event.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+1650):
items.body: A polished ball striker whose control and approach play make him a strong fit for a course that can punish wayward shots.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1800):
items.body: One of the most talented young players in golf, with enough power and precision to contend if his putter cooperates.

items.label: Collin Morikawa (+1800 to +2200):
items.body: An elite iron player whose ceiling rises on a course where approach shots matter as much as raw driving distance.

items.label: Patrick Cantlay (+2200):
items.body: A methodical, high-floor contender whose tee-to-green consistency suits a no-cut week at Quail Hollow.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2200):
items.body: A reliable all-around player with the patience and accuracy needed to stay in the mix.

items.label: Justin Thomas (+2500):
items.body: A former champion in this event and always a threat when his short game and creativity are sharp.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-player Signature Event with no cut.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $20 million, with $3.6 million to the winner.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Sepp Straka won the 2025 edition.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Quail Hollow Club, Charlotte, North Carolina.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: May 7 through May 10, 2026.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A long, demanding championship test that rewards distance, strong iron play, and composure on difficult holes.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-hole stroke play, no cut.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Standard PGA Tour Signature Event broadcast coverage is expected.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs May 7 through May 10, 2026.
